message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1). Venv: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
/tmp/pass1_reconfirm.json is a JSON array of 10 pass-1 findings that were strengthened after a first confirmation pass found them 'appropriate but incomplete'. Each item has: issue, title, file, problem, fix_update (what was just added/changed).
Constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign, no metered API, engine imports no adapter, lazy optional backends.

You are independent CONFIRMER #2 of 2 for INDEX 9 (0-based) in /tmp/pass1_reconfirm.json.
Read that item (issue, problem, fix_update). Then verify the CURRENT state on branch harden/pass-1: read the changed source AND the named regression test(s), and run a PoC / the specific tests against the venv where useful. Decide: appropriate = the fix soundly addresses the root cause (no hack/over-claim); complete = it fully resolves the finding AND has a regression test that would actually catch a regression (a test that passes equally with/without the fix does NOT count). If the item documents a deliberately-deferred scope (e.g. #119's Protocol-boundary note), judge completeness against that honestly-scoped fix, not an unbounded ideal. Set either false with specifics if not met. Echo the issue number.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content: 1	[{"issue": "102", "title": "run_json applies retries multiplicatively, causing a retry-storm and unbounded blocking time with no overall deadline", "file": "src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py", "sev": "HIGH", "problem": "`run_json` loops `MAX_RETRIES+1` times (line 531) and each iteration calls `_run_with_retries` (line 542), which itself retries up to `MAX_RETRIES` transient attempts AND up to `RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES` rate-limit attempts. The budgets multiply. PoC (transient errors every attempt): `_attempt` is invoked 9 times = (MAX_RETRIES+1)^2, not the intended ~3. Worse, the rate-limit path: per outer iteration the rate budget sleeps sum(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF*(r+1) for r in 0..4) = 450s; times 3 outer iteratio", "fix_update": "run_json now re-rolls ONLY on JSON parse failure (transient/rate/permanent propagate, no budget multiplication) + _sleep_bounded bounds inner backoff by the overall deadline. New tests: no-multiplication, seed re-roll on parse failure, deadline bound."}, {"issue": "111", "title": "Graph-orchestration gate decisions never enter the hash-chained provenance trail \u2014 the documented \u201cappend-only trail\u201d for the durable path is unchained, untamper-evident LangGraph state", "file": "/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py", "sev": "MEDIUM", "problem": "There are two governance mechanisms (ADR-0010): the synchronous run_study path records every gate decision into the hash-chained ProvenanceTrail (provenance.py), and the durable LangGraph interrupt/resume path (gatenode.make_gate_node) records decisions as plain dicts appended to a LangGraph state list 'gate_decisions'. GOVERNANCE \u00a73 states the chained ProvenanceTrail \"is the spine\u2026 No step can be retroactively removed or reordered,\" and GOVERNANCE.md lines 47-50 call the graph's gate_decisions ", "fix_update": "make_gate_node already accepts recorder= (bridges to ProvenanceTrail); now GOVERNANCE \u00a73 + graph.py docstring honestly scope that the durable graph path needs the recorder to be hash-chained. Test test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail."}, {"issue": "113", "title": "GateReview is a frozen dataclass whose Mapping payload is not frozen \u2014 mutable-in-place and unhashable (issue #7 escape)", "file": "src/assay_engine/pipeline.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "GateReview is declared @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True) with a `payload: Mapping[str, Any]` field but, unlike EVERY other frozen mapping-bearing value record in the engine (ProvenanceEntry, GateDecision, Corpus, ClaimRecord, Verdict, Hypothesis, BaselineArtifact, ReasoningRequest, etc.), it has no __post_init__ that normalizes the field through freeze_mapping. This reproduces both halves of the codebase's own documented issue #7 (see src/assay_engine/_frozen.py module docstring):\n\n1. Mutable-", "fix_update": "GateReview.__post_init__ freezes payload; NEW test test_gate_review_payload_is_frozen_and_hashable asserts FrozenDict + mutation raises + hashable."}, {"issue": "114", "title": "ProvenanceTrail.record performs a non-atomic check-then-act on shared list state (not thread-safe)", "file": "src/assay_engine/provenance.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "record() computes `seq = len(self._entries)` and `prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash` (read), then does work (clock, digest, freeze), then `self._entries.append(entry)` (write), with no lock. If a single ProvenanceTrail were shared across threads, two concurrent record() calls could read the same seq/prev_hash and append two entries that both claim e.g. seq=N with the same prev_hash, breaking the chain so verify_records() (which asserts e.seq == i and e.prev_hash == prev) would fail \u2014 a co", "fix_update": "record() lock-guarded; NEW test test_record_is_thread_safe_under_concurrency (8 threads x50, verify + contiguous seqs)."}, {"issue": "117", "title": "QdrantVectorStore owns and never closes its self-constructed client (connection/handle leak)", "file": "src/assay_engine/persistence/vectorstore.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "When no client is injected, `QdrantVectorStore.__init__` constructs its own `QdrantClient` via `get_qdrant_client()` (line 64) and stores it on `self._client`, but the class exposes no `close()`/context-manager and never closes the client. `QdrantClient` holds an underlying HTTP connection pool (httpx) / gRPC channel; for a self-constructed client the store is the sole owner, so the connection resources are leaked for the lifetime of every store instance with no deterministic release. Long-runni", "fix_update": "_owns_client + close()/context-manager; NEW test test_vector_store_closes_owned_client (owned client closed via get_qdrant_client + context exit)."}, {"issue": "118", "title": "QdrantVectorStore.upsert materializes all PointStructs (with per-vector copies) before sending any batch, so batching bounds payload but not memory", "file": "src/assay_engine/persistence/vectorstore.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "upsert builds the complete `points` list \u2014 one PointStruct per id, each holding `list(v)` (a fresh copy of the whole vector) \u2014 for the entire input before the chunking loop at line 88 sends anything. The comment claims chunking handles 'a large ingest', but it only bounds each HTTP request payload; peak process memory still holds the full corpus of vectors duplicated into PointStructs at once. For a real ingest (e.g. 5M docs x 768 float dims) this is the entire embedding set re-copied into Pytho", "fix_update": "upsert builds one batch at a time; NEW test test_vector_store_upsert_builds_one_batch_at_a_time asserts only batch_size vectors consumed per send (2,4,6 not 6,6,6)."}, {"issue": "119", "title": "corpus_fingerprint is recomputed redundantly per study (ingest + every build_baseline_artifact call), each a full canonical copy + sort + JSON of the whole corpus", "file": "src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "corpus_fingerprint builds a full list-comprehension copy of every unit and relation, sorts all units by id, canonicalizes every attribute mapping via _plain, and json.dumps the whole thing \u2014 O(total corpus bytes) time and memory. run_study computes it once at ingest (pipeline.py:280, `cfp`). Then build_baseline_artifact (determinism.py:104) computes it again from scratch for the determinism record's `input_hashes['corpus']` and `corpus_fingerprint`; a study's BaselineBuilder that calls build_bas", "fix_update": "build_baseline_artifact accepts corpus_hash (no recompute); NEW test test_build_baseline_reuses_precomputed_corpus_hash. NOTE: full e2e dedup across the BaselineBuilder Protocol boundary is deliberately deferred (would require a breaking build() signature change for a LOW perf item) \u2014 documented scope."}, {"issue": "120", "title": "Package version is hardcoded in two independent places (pyproject `version` and `__init__.__version__`), and the second feeds the reproducibility/provenance manifest", "file": "pyproject.toml", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "`pyproject.toml` sets `version = \"0.0.1\"` (line 7) while `src/assay_engine/__init__.py` separately hardcodes `__version__ = \"0.0.1\"` (line 28). These are two independent strings with no single source of truth \u2014 hatchling is not configured for dynamic versioning (`grep dynamic`/`hatch.version` returns nothing). This is not merely a style nit: `src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py` imports `__version__ as _ENGINE_VERSION` (line 24) and stamps it into the determinism/provenance record as `compo", "fix_update": "version dynamic + hatch.version path; NEW tests in test_packaging.py (no static version, dynamic declared, dist==__version__)."}, {"issue": "121", "title": "mypy is pinned to `python_version = \"3.11\"` but CI advertises and matrix-tests up to 3.14, so 3.12\u20133.14-only type errors are never caught", "file": "pyproject.toml", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "`[tool.mypy] python_version = \"3.11\"` forces mypy to type-check the code as if running on Python 3.11 regardless of the runner's interpreter. The core CI lane runs mypy across a 3.11\u20133.14 matrix and the integration lane runs it on 3.12, but because the analysis target is fixed at 3.11, type behavior that differs across versions (e.g. stdlib stubs that gained/changed signatures in 3.12+, version-gated typing constructs) is only ever validated at the 3.11 floor. The project classifies and matrix-t", "fix_update": "mypy python_version removed; NEW test test_mypy_is_not_pinned_to_a_single_python_version parses pyproject."}, {"issue": "122", "title": "run_study emits phase/reasoning spans but never starts tracing or stamps run_id baggage \u2014 traces cannot be correlated to the run or the experiment-tracker run_id", "file": "/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "run_study is the documented end-to-end composed runner and it opens trace spans for every phase (tracer.span('phase:INGEST') etc.) and the reasoning seam emits LLM/AGENT spans underneath. But run_study never calls bootstrap_tracing() and never wraps execution in run_trace_context(run_id) \u2014 grep of pipeline.py shows no reference to either. run_trace_context (tracing.py:145-163) is what stamps the 'assay.run_id' baggage onto every span so traces are correlatable across stores; the orchestration gr", "fix_update": "run_study calls bootstrap_tracing + wraps phases in run_trace_context(corr_id); NEW test test_run_study_correlates_spans_to_run_id asserts every phase span sees assay.run_id baggage + detached after."}]

content.text: Index 9 is issue #119. Let me read the relevant files.

content.content:
1	"""Determinism & reproducibility harness for the baseline (ADR-0001, METHODOLOGY.md §1, §7).
2	
3	A baseline is only a yardstick if the *same inputs reproduce the same baseline*. This module
4	makes that contract concrete and engine-level: it content-hashes inputs, derives seeds
5	deterministically from those hashes, records component versions, and stamps all of it into a
6	:class:`~assay_engine.baseline.toolkit.BaselineArtifact`'s ``determinism`` record — so a
7	hostile reviewer can confirm a baseline was produced exactly as claimed.
8	
9	It is dependency-free and pure. The heavy, *choice-bearing* builders (which embedding model,
10	which clustering algorithm, which graph construction) encode dataset/study decisions and are
11	supplied by a study's adapter as :class:`~assay_engine.baseline.toolkit.BaselineBuilder`
12	implementations — not prescribed by the engine (ADR-0002). This harness records *how* such a
13	builder ran so the result is reproducible regardless of which builder it was.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import hashlib
19	import json
20	import sys
21	from dataclasses import dataclass
22	from typing import Any, Mapping
23	
24	from assay_engine import __version__ as _ENGINE_VERSION
25	
26	# The canonical hashing primitives moved to ``assay_engine._canonical`` (see note below); these
27	# imports re-export them at their historical home so existing call sites keep working.
28	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_plain as _plain  # noqa: F401
29	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_bytes as hash_bytes  # noqa: F401  (re-export)
30	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_text
31	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_value as hash_value  # re-export (baseline public API)
32	from assay_engine._canonical import keytag as _keytag  # noqa: F401
33	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
34	from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact
35	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
36	
37	# The canonical hashing primitives (``_plain``/``_keytag``/``hash_bytes``/``hash_text``/
38	# ``hash_value``) live in ``assay_engine._canonical`` so they sit below both ``baseline`` and
39	# ``methodology`` in the import graph (audit #B1/#B2/#B5 originally landed here; hoisted so
40	# pre-registration can content-bind a hypothesis with the *same* type-faithful digest). They are
41	# re-exported above for back-compat.
42	
43	
44	def corpus_fingerprint(corpus: Corpus) -> str:
45	    """Deterministic content hash of a corpus (order-independent across units/relations)."""
46	    units = [
47	        {"id": u.unit_id, "text": u.text, "attrs": _plain(u.attributes)}
48	        for u in sorted(corpus.units, key=lambda u: u.unit_id)
49	    ]
50	    relations = [
51	        {"s": r.source_id, "t": r.target_id, "k": r.kind, "attrs": _plain(r.attributes)}
52	        for r in sorted(corpus.relations, key=lambda r: (r.source_id, r.target_id, r.kind))
53	    ]
54	    payload = {"units": units, "relations": relations, "metadata": _plain(corpus.metadata)}
55	    return hash_text(json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, allow_nan=False))
56	
57	
58	def stable_seed(*parts: str, bits: int = 32) -> int:
59	    """A deterministic non-negative integer seed derived from ``parts``.
60	
61	    Derived from a hash so the same inputs always yield the same seed — reproducible without a
62	    hard-coded magic number, and distinct inputs get distinct seeds.
63	    """
64	    digest = hashlib.sha256("\x1f".join(parts).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
65	    return int(digest, 16) % (1 << bits)
66	
67	
68	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
69	class DeterminismRecord:
70	    """The reproducibility provenance stamped onto a baseline (ADR-0001)."""
71	
72	    seed: int
73	    input_hashes: Mapping[str, str]
74	    component_versions: Mapping[str, str]
75	
76	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
77	        object.__setattr__(self, "input_hashes", freeze_mapping(self.input_hashes))
78	        object.__setattr__(self, "component_versions", freeze_mapping(self.component_versions))
79	
80	    def as_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
81	        return {
82	            "seed": self.seed,
83	            "input_hashes": dict(self.input_hashes),
84	            "component_versions": dict(self.component_versions),
85	        }
86	
87	
88	def build_baseline_artifact(
89	    corpus: Corpus,
90	    contents: Mapping[str, Any],
91	    *,
92	    component_versions: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
93	    extra_inputs: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
94	    seed: int | None = None,
95	    corpus_hash: str | None = None,
96	) -> BaselineArtifact:
97	    """Assemble a :class:`BaselineArtifact` with a complete determinism record.
98	
99	    ``contents`` is the builder's output (embeddings, similarity graph, clusters, …).
100	    ``component_versions`` records the builder + any model/library versions (the engine and
101	    Python versions are added automatically). ``extra_inputs`` are additional inputs to hash
102	    (e.g. config). ``seed``, if omitted, is derived deterministically from the corpus + inputs
103	    so it is reproducible. ``corpus_hash`` lets a caller pass an already-computed
104	    :func:`corpus_fingerprint` to avoid recomputing it (#119).
105	    """
106	    # Reuse a precomputed fingerprint if the caller already has one (e.g. the runner computed it
107	    # at ingest), avoiding a redundant full canonical-hash of the whole corpus (#119).
108	    corpus_hash = corpus_hash if corpus_hash is not None else corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
109	    if extra_inputs and "corpus" in extra_inputs:
110	        raise ValueError("'corpus' is a reserved input-hash key — rename the extra input (#B7)")
111	    input_hashes = {"corpus": corpus_hash}
112	    for name, value in (extra_inputs or {}).items():
113	        input_hashes[name] = hash_value(value)
114	
115	    versions = {"engine": _ENGINE_VERSION, "python": sys.version.split()[0]}
116	    versions.update(component_versions or {})
117	
118	    if seed is None:
119	        # Derive from all input hashes (the corpus hash is already among them under "corpus").
120	        seed = stable_seed(*(input_hashes[k] for k in sorted(input_hashes)))
121	
122	    record = DeterminismRecord(seed=seed, input_hashes=input_hashes, component_versions=versions)
123	    return BaselineArtifact(
124	        corpus_fingerprint=corpus_hash, contents=contents, determinism=record.as_dict()
125	    )
126

harden/pass-1

d0154e1 fix(harden-1): perf + graph-provenance batch (#106,#107,#111)
d9e5399 fix(harden-1): ops/supply-chain batch (#108,#120,#121)
6225498 fix(harden-1): provenance + observability batch (#109,#110,#113,#114,#119,#122)
5f4b3eb fix(harden-1): reliability + security batch (#104,#105,#112,#115,#116,#117,#118)

content.text: The fix adds a `corpus_hash` parameter so callers can avoid recompute. Now let me find the regression test and check the caller (pipeline.py) to assess the deferred-scope note.

271	def test_build_baseline_reuses_precomputed_corpus_hash(monkeypatch):
272	    # #119: a caller that already has the fingerprint can pass it to skip the redundant recompute
273	    import assay_engine.baseline.determinism as d
274	
275	    corpus = Corpus(units=(Unit("u0", "t0"), Unit("u1", "t1")))
276	    calls = {"n": 0}
277	    orig = d.corpus_fingerprint
278	
279	    def counting(c):
280	        calls["n"] += 1
281	        return orig(c)
282	
283	    monkeypatch.setattr(d, "corpus_fingerprint", counting)
284	    art = d.build_baseline_artifact(corpus, {"k": 1}, corpus_hash="deadbeef" * 8)
285	    assert art.corpus_fingerprint == "deadbeef" * 8 and calls["n"] == 0  # no recompute
286	    d.build_baseline_artifact(corpus, {"k": 1})  # without it → recomputes
287	    assert calls["n"] == 1
288	
289	
290	def test_freeze_keeps_ndarray_opaque_o1():
291	    # #107: a numpy array in baseline contents is frozen to a small (kind, shape, bytes)
292	    # descriptor — NOT a recursively tuple-ized cell-by-cell copy.
293	    np = pytest.importorskip("numpy")
294	    from assay_engine._frozen import freeze
295	
296	    frozen = freeze(np.zeros((100, 100)))
297	    # descriptor form: a 3-tuple (kind, shape, frozen-bytes), not a 100-deep nested structure
298	    assert isinstance(frozen, tuple) and len(frozen) == 3
299	    assert frozen[1] == (100, 100)
300

content.text: The test is meaningful: it asserts `corpus_fingerprint` is NOT called when `corpus_hash` is passed (calls n==0), and IS called when omitted (n==1). This would fail without the fix (without the parameter, it would always recompute). Let me verify the test passes, and check the deferred-scope claim by examining the pipeline.py caller.

content.text: Let me verify the deferred-scope claim. The pipeline at line 299 computes `cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)`, then at line 355 calls `plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, ...)` — the builder is a Protocol whose `build()` signature doesn't accept the precomputed hash. So the engine cannot pass `cfp` into the builder's internal `build_baseline_artifact` call without a breaking signature change.

43:class BaselineBuilder(Protocol):
46:    def build(

43	class BaselineBuilder(Protocol):
44	    """Build a :class:`BaselineArtifact` from a corpus, blind to any external claims."""
45	
46	    def build(
47	        self, corpus: Corpus, *, claim_guard: ClaimBlindGuard[ExternalClaimsSource]
48	    ) -> BaselineArtifact:
49	        """Construct the baseline. Must run with ``claim_guard.sealed()`` in force."""
50	        ...

content.text:
Confirmed. The `BaselineBuilder.build(corpus, *, claim_guard)` Protocol does not accept a precomputed corpus hash. So full e2e dedup across the runner→builder→`build_baseline_artifact` chain genuinely requires a breaking Protocol signature change, exactly as the fix_update's NOTE states. The deferral is honestly scoped for a LOW perf item.

Assessment against the honestly-scoped fix:
- Root cause (redundant recompute in `build_baseline_artifact`) is addressed: the function now accepts `corpus_hash` and reuses it via `corpus_hash if corpus_hash is not None else corpus_fingerprint(corpus)` (line 108), no hack.
- The `corpus` reserved-key guard (#B7) is preserved, and the seed derivation still uses the corpus hash correctly.
- The regression test asserts behavior that genuinely depends on the fix: 0 fingerprint calls when `corpus_hash` is supplied, 1 when omitted. Without the new parameter, `corpus_fingerprint` would always be invoked, so the `calls["n"] == 0` assertion would fail — the test would catch a regression. Verified passing in the venv.
- The deferred e2e dedup is real and correctly bounded by the Protocol boundary; judged against that scope, the finding is complete.

input.reasoning:
Confirmer #2 for INDEX 9 / issue #119 on branch harden/pass-1. The finding is that corpus_fingerprint is recomputed redundantly (O(total corpus bytes) full canonical copy + sort + JSON) every time build_baseline_artifact runs, in addition to the runner's ingest-time computation.

APPROPRIATE: The fix soundly addresses the root cause at the function it targets. build_baseline_artifact now takes an optional corpus_hash and at determinism.py:108 uses `corpus_hash if corpus_hash is not None else corpus_fingerprint(corpus)` — a clean reuse, not a hack. The #B7 reserved-key guard, input-hash assembly, and seed derivation (which reads the corpus hash from input_hashes) all remain correct. No over-claim: the docstring (lines 103-104) accurately describes the parameter as a caller-supplied precomputed fingerprint.

DEFERRED SCOPE (honestly judged): The fix_update NOTE deliberately defers full e2e dedup across the BaselineBuilder Protocol boundary, citing a breaking build() signature change. I verified this is genuine: the runner computes `cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)` (pipeline.py:299) but the only baseline construction path it can reach is plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, *, claim_guard) (pipeline.py:355), and BaselineBuilder.build (toolkit.py:46-50) accepts only (corpus, *, claim_guard) — no slot to thread cfp into the builder's internal build_baseline_artifact call. So eliminating the second compute end-to-end truly requires a breaking Protocol change, correctly bounded for a LOW perf item. Judged against this honestly-scoped fix (per the task instruction to judge deferred scope honestly), the engine-level redundancy in build_baseline_artifact itself is fully resolved.

COMPLETE: The regression test test_build_baseline_reuses_precomputed_corpus_hash (tests/test_baseline.py:271-287) monkeypatches corpus_fingerprint with a call counter and asserts calls['n'] == 0 when corpus_hash is supplied and calls['n'] == 1 when it is omitted. This test genuinely depends on the fix: without the new corpus_hash parameter the function would always call corpus_fingerprint, so the first assertion (n==0) would fail — i.e. it does NOT pass equally with/without the fix. It also asserts the supplied hash propagates to art.corpus_fingerprint. Verified passing in the named venv.</reasoning>

The deferred e2e dedup is genuine and honestly scoped — `BaselineBuilder.build` (toolkit.py:46-50) has no slot to thread the runner's precomputed `cfp` (pipeline.py:299,355) into the builder's internal call, so full dedup would require a breaking Protocol change for a LOW perf item.
